content: customData.geo is the canonical field name on ExcalidrawElement for geo-anchoring (NOT customData.geoAnchor). Shape is a discriminated union: {kind:'point'|'bbox'|'polyline',...,zRef:number}. Phase 3 and Phase 5 consumer tables contain documentation drift with flat shapes — authoritative definition is packages/geo/types.ts per tech spec §3.1.

name: schema-version-reserved-fields
description: GeoCustomData carries schemaVersion:1 and projection:'mercator' from v1. projection reserved for future globe/non-Mercator view. CoordinateSync asserts projection==='mercator' and throws otherwise as forward-compat sentinel. Schema changes post-v1 require migrations.
replaceAllElements is O(n) — captureUpdate:'never' only skips undo capture, not element replacement cost. Initial assumption that captureUpdate skipped all overhead was wrong.
resolution: Use incremental projection updates instead of full scene replacement. Phase 1 Task 16 benchmark gate validates budget.

description: map.project() assumed not perspective-correct under pitch — wrong. It IS perspective-correct. Runtime assertion workaround was the wrong fix.
resolution: Set maxPitch:0 in MapLibre constructor options. No runtime correction needed.

description: Excalidraw concurrent same-id LWW described in plan as not converging — wrong. It DOES converge via versionNonce tiebreak. Plan description was incorrect.
resolution: No fix needed. versionNonce tiebreak provides LWW convergence. Update mental model.

content: Use 'import type { Map as MapLibreMap }' from maplibre-gl in packages that consume MapLibre types — keeps the package test-friendly without pulling in the full GL runtime as a hard dep. Declare maplibre-gl as devDep + optional peerDep in package.json.

title: CoordinateSync exposes both per-element helpers and bulk orchestrator — not duplicates
rationale: geoToScene/sceneToGeo are stateless per-element projection primitives used by individual anchor render paths. syncMapToScene is the bulk orchestrator owning the perf budget (8ms frame budget) and undo invariants (all elements update atomically in one Excalidraw batch). Worker B shipped only the per-element split; plan required the bulk orchestrator. Ruling out collapsing them: a single method cannot satisfy both the stateless per-element transform contract AND the dirty-tracking + batching + undo-history invariants simultaneously. They are structurally different layers.

name: camera-event-driven-react-hook
description: Map camera-event-driven React hook structure: (1) useMemo builds CoordinateSync instance only when (map, api) tuple changes — both must be non-null; (2) useEffect registers all camera events via const CAMERA_EVENTS = ['move','zoom','rotate','pitch'] array; (3) lodash.throttle standalone package (NOT 'lodash') wraps handler at 16ms with {leading:true, trailing:true}; (4) cleanup calls handler.cancel() before map.off() in symmetry, then sync.detach(); (5) sync.attach()/detach() lifecycle seam must be called explicitly — CoordinateSync does not self-attach; (6) event handler stored as const at hook scope — never anonymous arrow in add/remove or identity breaks. Reference: code/apps/atlas-app/src/hooks/useCoordinateSync.ts.

name: bench-dominantSegment-unreliable-sub-ms
description: Bench dominantSegment heuristic is unreliable when both timing segments are sub-millisecond — wrapping map.project with performance.now() per call inflates the residual segment (non-project work) when project_ms is <1ms. In the phase-1-baseline bench, dominantSegment came back 'updateScene' (the mocked no-op) because project_ms couldn't accumulate above the measurement noise floor. The bench's pass/fail gate (total.p99 < 8ms budget) is unaffected by this noise. dominantSegment values in bench results are unreliable when both segments are sub-ms; interpret only total.p99 for gate decisions. Reference: code/packages/geo/bench/results/phase-1-baseline.json.

content: scaleMode 9-cell dispatch matrix for _projectElement: GeoAnchor.kind (point|bbox|polyline) x ScaleMode (geographic|screen|hybrid). Rules: point+geographic — scale el.width/el.height by computeScaleFactor; point+screen — no scaling; point+hybrid — scale width/height by factor; bbox+geographic — project span from stored coords; bbox+screen — override projected span with stored pixel span; bbox+hybrid — clamp span to 2^-2..2^+2 of reference; polyline+geographic — reproject all points; polyline+screen — no change to points; polyline+hybrid — reproject but clamp segment lengths. Source: code/packages/geo/src/scaleMode.ts + CoordinateSync.ts wired in T17 (commit 8579bc6).
